en.title: Traditional Chinese medicine
en.prompt: Please play the role of a senior traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) practitioner who is also a nutrition expert. Based on the symptoms I describe, analyze the possible causes. From the TCM perspective, provide acupuncture and moxibustion treatment plans, and recommend a specific herbal formula. Detail the dosage and therapeutic effects of each ingredient. From a nutrition standpoint, provide corresponding nutritional supplement recommendations, specifying the nutrients to be supplemented and their appropriate dosages. My symptoms are [describe specific physical symptoms].
en.remark: Traditional Chinese medicine diagnosis involves multiple factors, and the treatment plan provided is solely for reference purposes. Specific prescriptions should be provided by a licensed physician. Contributed by @dong8531.

related.title: Unrestricted ChatGPT
related.remark: 2023.06.10 Downgraded, unable to fully hack. Unlock ChatGPT 3.5 developer mode. Contributed by @Songxuan11.

related.title: Succubus
related.remark: ⚠️Before using this cue, it is necessary to unlock the developer mode, allowing the AI to portray a seductive character, which is particularly suitable for intimate plotlines in literature. Contributed by @mrdog233o5.

related.title: Nature Style Editing
related.remark: Polish according to the style of Nature, or provide a writing style to emulate. Contributed by @Pfyuan77.

metaDescription: Health issues? Get TCM diagnosis, acupuncture plans, and herbal formulas. Combine with nutritional advice for holistic health.

faq.q: Can I take the AI's TCM prescription directly to a pharmacy?
faq.a: Absolutely not. TCM requires differentiation-based treatment; identical symptoms with different constitutions take completely different herbs. AI prescriptions draw from generic case books with imprecise dosages and sometimes include toxic herbs (aconite, wolfsbane, nux vomica). Have a licensed TCM doctor write prescriptions.

faq.q: Is it useful as an entry-level TCM study tool?
faq.a: Yes. Useful for understanding terminology (yin deficiency, qi stagnation, phlegm-dampness) and classical prescription structure. But the AI frequently misquotes classics like Shang Han Lun or Jin Gui Yao Lue. For advanced study rely on authoritative textbooks and famous physicians' writings.
